name: Mandala
essence: Mandala means 'circle'. A geometry that has a centre and a periphery, containing all directions. Jung says the mandala is the purest sign of the Self — when the psyche orders itself it draws a mandala.
symbolism: Centre = essence, circle = wholeness, four / eight directions = the field of archetypes. Watching a mandala is meditative because the eye sees order yet infinity.
dreamMeaning: A mandala, a symmetrical pattern within a circle, a flower form, a group of four, or a scene that gives the feeling 'everything in its place' in a dream — a healing dream from the Self. It usually comes during crisis or transformation; the message is 'the centre is firm, continue'.
wakingMeaning: Has your life scattered? Drawing a mandala (even doodling) recalls inner order. Do it as ritual, not as art. Tibetan monks make them for weeks then blow them away — wholeness is a process, not an artwork.
advice: Today draw a mandala. It doesn't matter if it's bad. Start at the centre, open outward. When done, look at it 5 minutes.

name: Serpent (Ouroboros)
essence: Ouroboros, the snake biting its tail. From Egypt to Greece, China to alchemy — there is a form everywhere. Symbol of endless transformation. The snake sheds its skin; all forms die and live again.
symbolism: The snake has two faces: both venom and medicine. The medical symbol (Asclepius's staff) is a serpent. The serpent who casts you out of paradise; the cobra that protects Buddha. Shadow and healing in the same being.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a snake in a dream can be frightening but is rarely a bad sign. Most often a transformation is coming — the old skin falls. A snake biting you: the painful awakening you need. A snake around you: protection or encirclement. White snake: sacred message. Colour matters.
wakingMeaning: Is an area of life wanting to 'shed skin'? When the serpent energy opens old habits become itchy; you cannot rest without letting go. Fear is normal — the snake always brings transformation.
advice: Today pick one 'old skin' (habit, relationship, object). Just become consciously aware. The snake doesn't rush; it drops at the right moment.

name: Tree (Tree of Life)
essence: The Tree of Life exists in almost every culture: Sefirot in Kabbalah, Tuba in Islam, the Tree of the Realm in Turkic. Roots in the underworld (unconscious), branches in the sky (superconscious), trunk the human.
symbolism: A tree carries both change (seasons) and continuity (centuries). The balance between root and branch is the map of existence. Sitting under a tree (Buddha) — the natural scene of enlightenment.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a tree in dreams is generally a good omen. A healthy tree says your life is rooted. A dry / cut tree: an area needs renewal. Climbing a tree — striving to reach a goal. Sitting under a tree — turning inward.
wakingMeaning: If your life's roots (family, tradition, body) are weak the branches (plans, dreams) break quickly. Or the opposite: overly rooted. Remember the tree's balance: root and branch grow in equal proportion.
advice: Today touch a tree. Its trunk isn't cold; it's alive. Stand for a minute. Ask: am I as rooted and as free?

name: Water (Ocean, River)
essence: Water is the symbol of the unconscious in every culture. It flows, takes shape, is deep, hides the unseen. 'You cannot enter the same river twice' (Heraclitus) — so too is the unconscious.
symbolism: Ocean = collective unconscious. River = life flow. Lake = calm inner. Flood = uncontrolled outpouring of repressed feelings. Ice = frozen feeling. Steam = spiritual ascent.
dreamMeaning: The colour and state of water in a dream are very important. Clear water = emotional clarity. Cloudy water = confusion. Flood = uncontrolled spilling of feelings. Diving — conscious descent into the unconscious, usually good. Being able to breathe under water — adapting.
wakingMeaning: Are you thirsty? Emotional? If the water element is low you become bodily hardened and emotionally frozen. Bath, sea, crying, drinking a glass of water consciously — small rituals.
advice: Today drink a glass of water just as water. Taste it, see it. Imagine it as fluid consciousness. Water always helps.

name: Moon
essence: Moon has no light of its own — it reflects. Feminine energy, cycle, feeling. Its 28-day cycle syncs with the female body; the tides with the ocean.
symbolism: New moon = intention, beginning. Full moon = completion, exposure. Crescent = growth or decrease. Dark moon = turning inward, unconscious.
dreamMeaning: Seeing the moon in a dream is usually an emotional message. Full moon = fullness, perhaps an awareness. Waning moon = something incomplete. Blood moon = deep change. Looking at the moon: looking at the unconscious.
wakingMeaning: Have you noticed you sync with the moon cycle? Many people sleep less, are more emotional during the full moon. This is not coincidence. Follow your moon cycle; biological and psychological.
advice: Today approach a window at night, observe the moon's phase. 1 minute. The phase you're in informs you. New moon is open to intention, full moon to sharing.

name: Sun
essence: The sun emits its own light. Masculine energy, will, consciousness, the best side of the ego. In most mythologies the highest god: Ra, Apollo, Surya.
symbolism: Rising sun = new beginning. Full sun = power and visibility. Setting sun = transition from consciousness to unconscious. Hidden sun (cloudy) = depressive or repressed consciousness.
dreamMeaning: Seeing the sun in dreams is usually positive — clear consciousness, success, visibility. Dark sun or eclipse = consciousness shadow, crisis. Holding the sun in your palm = reaching your own power.
wakingMeaning: Does an area of life need 'light'? Do you want to be seen, or does being seen scare you? The sun question: how much do you allow yourself to be seen?
advice: Today look at the sun 2 minutes (not directly, its brightness). Vitamin D is a bodily and spiritual need in the age of deficiency. The sun sees you.

name: Mountain
essence: Mountain is where human meets sky. Sinai, Olympus, Kailash, Ararat — sacred mountains. Symbol of spiritual ascent and difficulty at once.
symbolism: Slope = struggle. Summit = the reached moment. Foot = beginning. Descending the mountain = sharing, returning to the ordinary.
dreamMeaning: Climbing a mountain in dreams is the symbol of the goal journey. Reaching the summit success, not reaching does not mean obstacle — process matters. Getting lost in the mountains — chasing a goal without knowing the way.
wakingMeaning: Is there a 'mountain' in your life? (Big goal, difficulty.) Mountain warning: while focusing on the summit don't forget to live the slope. The journey is as valuable as arrival.
advice: Today you can see a mountain / hill in the distance with your eyes. Look 30 seconds. If your body were as steady as a mountain, your mind as high — how would you feel?

name: Cave
essence: The cave is both womb and tomb. The shaman's initiation site, the prophet's place of retreat, the place where truth is kept. There is a treasure in its darkness.
symbolism: Bare cave = primal origin. Ornate cave (tomb) = honoured place of the dead. A cave with a light inside = inner consciousness. Muhammad in Hira, Jesus in the rock tomb — sacred caves.
dreamMeaning: Entering a cave in dreams is the sign of conscious descent to the unconscious. Fear in the darkness is normal. Seeing a light in the cave — the Self's call. Coming out of the cave — rebirth.
wakingMeaning: Do you need to turn inward? If society says 'be social' but you want a cave, listen. The clinical difference between solitude and seclusion: intention and return.
advice: Today create 20 minutes alone time. No phone. This is the modern cave. An answer is born in the dark.

name: Bridge
essence: A bridge unites two sides but belongs to neither. In Sufism the 'Sırat bridge' is thinner than a hair, sharper than a sword — to be crossed for arrival.
symbolism: Bridge = liminal area (threshold). When you step to one side your old life ends; in the middle you are suspended. The symbol of passing from one life-stage to another.
dreamMeaning: Crossing a bridge in dreams heralds a great transition. How solid the bridge, how difficult the crossing — questions. A dream where you stop in the middle and cannot go back: you are postponing a decision.
wakingMeaning: Are you at a transition threshold (job change, breakup, move)? The middle of the bridge is not pleasant nor scary — just cross. Don't look back, look ahead.
advice: Today think of a transition you've postponed. You may be stuck because you don't take the first step. Take the smallest step: an email, a phone call, a 'yes'.

name: Door / Threshold
essence: Every door separates one area from another. In ancient cultures the threshold was sacred — Hermes the god of doors, Janus the two-faced door god.
symbolism: Open door = opportunity, invitation. Closed door = obstacle or not yet. Locked door = an unconscious area. If you are finding a key — empowerment.
dreamMeaning: Opening / closing a door in dreams is a big symbol. A door you can't open: you aren't ready yet. A door leading into a room you don't know: an area discovered in the unconscious. Losing the key: your authority is being questioned.
wakingMeaning: Has a 'door' appeared in your life lately? (An offer, opportunity, call.) The door may be temporary. Be careful, but don't stay at the threshold too long — doors close.
advice: Today list 'open door' opportunities (3 of them). You may not have approached any. Pick one, take a small step today.

name: Star
essence: A star is light from afar — the sailor's compass, the Magi's guide. The fixed point needed to find direction in darkness.
symbolism: One star = personal destiny. Star cluster = community or family. Shooting star = momentary opportunity, wish. Five-pointed star = human figure (Vitruvian). Six-pointed = balance (Seal of Solomon).
dreamMeaning: Seeing a star in a dream is a message of hope and direction. A star dream at a moment of being lost says 'continue, there is a road'. Reaching a star — your big dream is close to coming true.
wakingMeaning: Do you have a 'compass star' in your life — a value, goal, person? Without it finding direction in darkness is hard. The star does not always have to be visible; knowing it is enough.
advice: Today write your life's 'north star' (highest value or goal) in one word. That word opens doors in darkness.

name: Egg
essence: An egg is unlimited potential pressed into a tiny shell. In many creation myths the world emerges from an egg.
symbolism: The egg's outer shell (persona), inner yolk (essence), white (nourishing field). Incubation time is a metaphor for patient waiting.
dreamMeaning: Seeing an egg in a dream is the sign of a new beginning — a project, a relationship, an intention about to be born. A broken egg — the shell opening, time. An egg in an incubator — a maturing requiring patience.
wakingMeaning: Is something 'incubating' inside you? A new idea, the seed of a dream? An egg needs patience; if you break it early it comes out incomplete. Wait until the time comes.
advice: Today think of an idea you've postponed saying 'it's not time yet'. Accept that it is in its incubation period. Let go of haste.

name: Heart
essence: The heart is not only an organ — in old texts it is the centre of intelligence and love. In Sufism the 'eye of the heart' is deeper than the mind's eye.
symbolism: Solid heart = wholeness. Broken heart = opening (Rumi: 'from the broken heart enters the light'). Bleeding heart = continued empathy. Iron heart = emotional freezing.
dreamMeaning: Touching the heart in a dream / heart pain / a bleeding heart is the unconscious's message about emotions. Symbolic 'heart surgery' means healing. The heart not beating is rarely about death; it is usually a warning of emotional closure.
wakingMeaning: The physical sensations of your heartbeat (chest tightness, burning) are linked to emotional loads. The body speaks before consciousness. Listen to your heart, both physically and spiritually.
advice: Today place your hand over your heart for a minute. Just feel your breath. Say 'I see you' to the centre of your body.

name: Eye (Third Eye)
essence: The eye is the organ of seeing but also the symbol of being seen and unseen. Third eye = the centre of intuition. Eye of Horus = divine protection.
symbolism: Open eye = awareness. Closed eye = turning inward or denial. Crying eye = feeling. Single eye = one perspective. Eye that doesn't want to see = limited perception.
dreamMeaning: Feeling watched by an eye in a dream: the unconscious observing you. Opening of the third eye: intuitive awakening. Losing the eye / going blind: not wanting to see a reality.
wakingMeaning: Did you choose to 'not see' something lately? The third eye sees first what it doesn't want to see. If you constantly oppose intuition with 'illogical', you are closing it.
advice: Today close your eyes for 5 minutes. How does information come in the world without images? This is the third-eye exercise.

name: Spiral
essence: The spiral both rotates and advances — not flat repetition but helical evolution. DNA, galaxy, shell, hurricane — always spirals. Found on the walls of ancient Neolithic temples.
symbolism: Spiralling inward = withdrawal, meditation. Spiralling outward = expansion. Turning on the same point but each time at another layer = inner maturing.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a spiral in dreams, a hurricane, being on a Ferris wheel, a spiral staircase — signs of the individuation journey. You may be taking the same lesson again — but this time at another level.
wakingMeaning: Are you living a different version of the same issue in life? This is not cycle but spiral. Each repetition comes from a higher layer. You weren't defeated — you grew.
advice: Today draw a spiral on paper, inward to outward. With each turn name a thing you learned. At the top of the spiral is today's you.

name: Cross / Cross-shape
essence: The cross unites four directions around a central point. Before the Christian cross it also existed in neolithic cultures. Horizontal = worldly, vertical = celestial; where they intersect is the human.
symbolism: Left-right = past-future. Up-down = sky-earth. Centre = now, me. That is why the cross is not only a religious but an existential symbol.
dreamMeaning: An intersection point in a dream, the parting of four directions, a cross symbol, or crossing shapes herald an existential decision. You are approaching the moment to 'choose a direction'.
wakingMeaning: Is there a 'cross' in your life — an intersection between horizontal (social, external) and vertical (spiritual, inner)? Neither alone is enough without the other.
advice: Today draw a cross on paper. On the horizontal write your outer goals, on the vertical your inner values. What meets at the centre?

name: Key
essence: A key is the authority to open the hidden. It opens a door but also means 'the owner is authorised'. Hermetic tradition likens 'gnosis' to a key.
symbolism: Finding a key = discovering inner authority. Losing a key = forgetting authority. Multiple keys = access to different areas. Old key = ancient knowledge.
dreamMeaning: Finding, losing a key, opening a lock in a dream — a big symbol. The key is usually the message of 'I authorise you' from the unconscious. Wrong key — you are trying the wrong solution.
wakingMeaning: Is there a locked area in your life? Have you forgotten that you have an authority? The key is usually hidden in plain sight — in the unconscious, in a simple sentence.
advice: Today on a long-stuck matter ask 'what have I forgotten?'. The key is usually in remembering.

name: Bird / Flight
essence: Bird is bridge between earth and sky. The symbol of the soul in many cultures. 'The soul is the cage of the bird' Rumi says; Sufis tell of the soul bird flying to the sky.
symbolism: White dove = peace, message. Black raven = messenger but mysterious. Eagle = high view, royalty. Owl = wisdom. Nightingale = art. Huma = luck.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a bird in a dream is a message from your spirit world. Caged bird = trapped spirit. Flying bird = liberation. Dead bird = end of a period (not necessarily bad — change). The species of bird gives detail.
wakingMeaning: Is there an area where you are stuck? The Bird archetype says 'you can fly' but first recognise the cage. Most cages are open; the bird just stands inside from habit.
advice: Today watch a bird through the window (1 minute). Feel yourself in its body. Where would you fly?

name: Fire
essence: Fire both creates and destroys. It cooks, warms, gives light; also burns. Sacred in Zoroastrianism, the main element of the Hindu altar.
symbolism: Stove fire = family, warmth. Camp fire = sharing. Candle = individual prayer. Wildfire = uncontrolled passion / anger. Ash = honouring what ends.
dreamMeaning: The tone of fire in dreams is important. Warm bright fire = enlivenment. Uncontrolled wildfire = bursting repressed energy. Ash = an ending. Carrying fire = becoming the carrier of enlightenment.
wakingMeaning: Is there a 'snuffed out' fire inside (passion, creativity, interest)? Or the opposite — an uncontrolled flame? Fire must always be balanced; candle suffices, wildfire destroys.
advice: Today light a candle, watch for 5 minutes. The flame moves constantly but has a centre. So do you.

name: Witches' Cauldron / Crucible
essence: The cauldron is both kitchen and magical tool. In Celtic myth Cerridwen's cauldron produces wisdom. In alchemy the crucible is the laboratory of transformation.
symbolism: Cast into the cauldron = the old delivered. Cooking = what is transforming. Smoke = what is revealed. Coming out of the cauldron = the new form.
dreamMeaning: A cauldron, soup, mixture, cooking process in a dream is the sign of an inner transformation. Something is 'cooking' — an intention, idea, relationship. Patience.
wakingMeaning: An intention not yet emerged? It is in the cauldron. Don't try to take it out early; before it is fully cooked its flavour isn't right. Patience is cooking time.
advice: Today prepare a meal patiently. Set an intention while cooking: 'I respect the time of whatever is maturing inside me'.

name: Labyrinth
essence: The classical labyrinth has a single path — you don't get lost, you go to the centre and come back. In medieval cathedrals (Chartres) it was walked instead of pilgrimage. Not just outer but inner journey.
symbolism: Maze (with multiple paths) and labyrinth are different: maze is trickery, labyrinth is process. Walking a labyrinth is meditation — step by step to the centre, then back.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a labyrinth in dreams means questioning the path of life itself. The fear of getting lost — actually forgetting that you are on the way.
wakingMeaning: Is there a 'where am I going?' fear in your life? Labyrinth says the path may not be visible but there is only one path. Keep walking step by step.
advice: Today draw a finger labyrinth (on paper). Slowly to centre with your finger, then back. 3 minutes. Your inside softens.

name: Clock / Time
essence: The clock is a human invention but consciousness of time is ancient. The hourglass tells everything flows away; the gear-clock that the cycle keeps turning.
symbolism: Flowing sand = time that does not return. Stopped clock = frozen or rescued moment. All clocks at 12:00 = aligned time. Wrong time = synchronisation problem.
dreamMeaning: Clock stopping in a dream, seeing the wrong time, missing a train is the sign of trouble in your relationship with time. The clock running backward — doubt about a bond to the past.
wakingMeaning: Does time rule you, or do you rule time? Is the feeling 'I can't keep up' constant? The clock question: are your priorities really yours or inherited?
advice: Today spend 1 hour 'timelessly'. No clock, no plan. This is a modern luxury and also a need.

name: Book
essence: A book is humanity's memory and also the metaphor of each individual's inner world. 'The book of my life' is not by chance.
symbolism: Closed book = unread potential. Open book = shared knowledge. Old book = inheritance. Burning book = lost knowledge. Book written = expression.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a book in a dream is usually a call of consciousness. Book in an unknown language = an unconscious message. Worn book = old wound. New book = new period.
wakingMeaning: Is there a book you haven't read lately? Is a book calling you? This is no coincidence — the right book comes at the right time.
advice: Today go to the bookshelf, close your eyes, pull a book. Open a random page. Let that sentence be your message today.

name: Salt
essence: Salt is essential for life (for the body) and at the same time protective (preserves food). In antiquity wages were even given as salt ('salary' from this word).
symbolism: Salt at the threshold = protection. Salt on food = flavour. Turning to salt (Lot's wife) = stuck and frozen on the past. Salty tears — mourning.
dreamMeaning: Seeing salt in dreams is a symbol of protection or purification. Spilling salt — loss of purity or renewal. Eating salt — tasting truth.
wakingMeaning: Salt balance in your body is important (in the modern sense). Symbolically — 'has your salt gone'? Can you still be meaningful, savoury? Salt is passion.
advice: Today consciously add salt to your meal. With the intention 'I add this much passion to my life'. Small ritual, big direction.

name: Wheel (Cycle)
essence: The wheel is the symbol of motion but also of cycle. The Buddhist Dharma wheel, Rome's Fortuna wheel, Tarot's Wheel of Fortune.
symbolism: When the wheel turns the one above will be below, the one below will be above. The idiom 'the wheel turns' — there is no permanent top.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a wheel in dreams, a Ferris wheel, a gear means a turn has begun in your life. The wheel broken — not turning — an area is blocked.
wakingMeaning: If your life is at the top — to avoid bitter surprise; if at the bottom — to not despair: the wheel turns. This state is temporary.
advice: Today recall a 'top' and a 'bottom' moment. Both passed. Now will pass too. This is comforting.

name: Bread
essence: Bread is the symbol of basic nourishment but 'eating bread together' (companion = the one with bread) is the symbol of sociality. Jesus says 'I am the bread of life'.
symbolism: Whole bread = togetherness. Broken bread = sharing. Burnt bread = neglect. Leavened bread = patience, transformation.
dreamMeaning: Seeing bread in dreams is a sign of abundance and the sustainability of daily life. Giving bread to someone — empathy. Being hungry — a basic need is being suppressed.
wakingMeaning: The idiom 'bread money' is no accident. Is your livelihood solid, do you share? Bread is both essential and sacred.
advice: Today eat bread consciously. Offer to someone. Nourishment multiplies when shared.

name: Nakedness
essence: Nakedness is both purity (Adam-Eve in paradise) and vulnerability (shame after being expelled from paradise). Symbolically the dropping of the mask.
symbolism: Conscious nakedness = authenticity. Forced nakedness = trauma. Not noticing your own nakedness = unconsciousness (innocent state). Art nakedness = raw reality.
dreamMeaning: Being naked in dreams is very common. Usually fear of 'being seen' — as if something you aren't ready for will be shared. Or the opposite — you want to be accepted as you really are.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel 'naked' (vulnerable) in some area of life? This is not weakness actually — real bond only happens when naked.
advice: Today share something genuine with someone (a fear, a dream). Small nakedness, big bond.

name: Jewel / Crystal
essence: Crystal forms under pressure and time in the earth. Carbon over millions of years turns into diamond. Pain transforms — the lesson of crystals.
symbolism: Crystal = purified essence. Jewel = value. Crystal hidden in the earth = inner treasure. Cracked diamond = alternative to perfectionism.
dreamMeaning: Finding a jewel in dreams is the noticing of an inner treasure. Crystal offered — your authority is being accepted. Losing a jewel — forgetting your value.
wakingMeaning: How much do you know your worth? If 'I am not valuable' is inside instead of crystal — you don't see the crystal underground. But it is there.
advice: Today list 3 valuable sides of yourself. Accept as they are. This is collecting crystal.

name: Lotus
essence: Lotus roots in mud, opens spotless above the water. The basic flower of Hindu and Buddhist symbolism. Without mud (pain) the lotus does not open.
symbolism: Closed lotus = potential. Opening lotus = spiritual development. Thousand-petaled lotus (sahasrara) = highest consciousness. Root in mud = the dirty side of life.
dreamMeaning: A lotus in a dream, a flower on the water, beauty rising from mud is the sign of spiritual opening. You passed through a difficult period — now you are opening.
wakingMeaning: Do you carry past pains as 'shame'? Lotus says: that mud is its root, the source of its flower. Without it you couldn't open.
advice: Today say to a past pain 'I grew thanks to you'. Think which 'mud' nourished your present beauty.

name: Butterfly
essence: The butterfly lives life through the caterpillar-cocoon-melting-butterfly transformation. In ancient Greek 'psyche' is both soul and butterfly. The soul's transformation.
symbolism: Caterpillar = old state. Cocoon = crisis, inner dissolution. Butterfly = new form. Knowing you melt in the cocoon = not depression but transformation.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a butterfly in a dream is the herald of completed transformation. Dead butterfly — end of a transition. Unable to leave the cocoon — needs more time.
wakingMeaning: Is there a feeling of 'melting' in your life? The old you is gone, the new you not yet? This is the cocoon period. Patience — butterfly is automatic.
advice: Today accept what your 'old state' was. Even if 'new state' isn't clear. The cocoon is temporary.

name: Eye of Horus (Wadjet)
essence: Horus's eye is Egypt's most powerful protective symbol. Torn apart by Set, reassembled by Thoth — it carries the power to heal the wound.
symbolism: Right eye Ra's (sun, consciousness), left eye Horus's (moon, intuition). Its six parts are read as the six senses. In modern mathematics 'Horus fractions'.
dreamMeaning: A big eye in dreams, being watched, an oracular gaze is the Wadjet image. A moment when you feel divinely protected.
wakingMeaning: Did you suddenly feel 'protected' in the middle of danger? Wadjet says: this is real. You are not alone.
advice: Today put a symbolic 'eye' in your pocket (necklace, sticker, Hamsa). Invisible protection strengthens when made visible.

name: Hamsa — Hand of Fatima
essence: Hamsa is a five-finger hand symbol. In Islam 'Hand of Fatima', in Judaism 'Hand of Miriam'. Protection from the evil eye — especially from the jealous gaze.
symbolism: The number 5 — the 5 prayers, 5 senses, 5 elements. The eye in the centre of the palm says 'I see you' against the evil eye. Blue colour traditional.
dreamMeaning: A hand in a dream, an opening palm, a blue eye is the Hamsa image. Call to protection from jealous gazes.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel 'heavy energies' coming from your environment? Hamsa says: the protection is real. Build a conscious barrier.
advice: Today think of a 'jealous energy' source in your life. Set a boundary with it — physically or mentally.

name: Yin-Yang
essence: Yin (black, feminine, night) and Yang (white, masculine, day) — each contains a dot of the other. Opposites both contain and transform each other.
symbolism: In continuous flow — one's peak is another's beginning. No absolute distinction. Every light carries the seed of darkness in itself, every darkness the seed of light.
dreamMeaning: A black-white cycle in a dream, two figures following each other is the Yin-Yang image. There is a balance issue inside.
wakingMeaning: Does opposition tire you in life? Yin-Yang says: don't separate one from the other — each is inside the other.
advice: Today reframe something you see as 'opposite' as 'complementary'. Don't speak it — just think.

name: Sefirot — Jewish Tree of Life
essence: Sefirot — 10 divine qualities connected by 22 paths. The Kabbalist tree of life shows the stages of creation and the human's road to the divine.
symbolism: Top to bottom: Keter (crown), Hochma, Bina, Hesed, Gevura, Tiferet, Netzah, Hod, Yesod, Malkhut (kingdom). Three columns: mercy, severity, balance.
dreamMeaning: A tree with 10 points in a dream, a ladder full of light is the Sefirot image. The map of the spiritual path opens.
wakingMeaning: Are you seeking a path on the spiritual road? Sefirot says: the path exists, sequential and conscious. Not at once but in stages.
advice: Today think of your preference between three columns (mercy, discipline, balance) in some area. Which dominates?

name: Vegvisir — Norse Compass
essence: Vegvisir means 'wayshower'. An 8-armed symbol — for finding the way even in a storm. Popular Viking and Norse tattoo in modern culture.
symbolism: 8 arms = 8 directions. Each arm points to a different road, a different decision. Even in darkness the symbol directs the person.
dreamMeaning: A compass in a dream, an 8-pointed star, finding the way in a storm is the Vegvisir image. Despite feeling lost, the direction is inside.
wakingMeaning: Difficulty finding direction? Vegvisir says: the compass is inside — there are 8 directions, trust your intuition.
advice: Today look at a decision from 8 directions: time, relationship, body, meaning, economy, health, future, past. Respect all sides.

name: Sankofa — Looking Back to the Future
essence: The Sankofa bird's body looks forward but its head looks back, holding a seed. It says 'it is not shame to take from behind'. An invitation to learn from ancestors, to be nourished from the root.
symbolism: The lesson taken from behind to go forward. African wisdom honouring the importance of history.
dreamMeaning: A bird with its head turned back in a dream, an old jewel being retrieved is the Sankofa image. You are taking a lesson from your ancestors.
wakingMeaning: Maybe you aren't fixated on the past but learning from the past also has value. Sankofa says: take the lesson, then let go.
advice: Today identify a 'seed' (teaching) from the past. How will you sow it into today?

name: Triskelion — Triple Spiral
essence: The triskelion is three spirals turning from one point. Past-present-future; birth-death-rebirth; mind-body-spirit. One of the most common ancient Celtic symbols.
symbolism: Triple motion — continuous change. The three arms emerging from the same point represent essential unity. Life, death, rebirth.
dreamMeaning: A triple spiral in a dream, three turning figures, a three-dimensional motion is the Triskelion image. A three-layered cycle of yours is opening.
wakingMeaning: In an area of your life are three layers (e.g. action, feeling, meaning) not aligning? Triskelion says: they come from the same point — align.
advice: Today ask three questions about a decision: What does my body say? What does my mind say? What does my soul say? If three align, the decision is there.

name: Bagua — Eight Trigrams
essence: Bagua is the 8 trigrams of I Ching — sky, earth, water, fire, mountain, lake, thunder, wind. Summarises every state of the universe in 8 symbols.
symbolism: Each trigram consists of 3 lines (solid yang, broken yin). When 8 combine 64 hexagrams arise. The basis of feng shui.
dreamMeaning: An 8-direction symbol in a dream, three changing lines is the Bagua image. You see different layers of a situation.
wakingMeaning: Are you trying to classify a situation? Bagua says: there are 8 main states. Which?
advice: Today think of an area of your life — which of the 8 trigrams is it closest to? (Sky = openness, mountain = stable, lake = joy...) Recognising is enough.

name: Vajra — Diamond Lightning
essence: Vajra (Dorje in Tibetan) means both 'diamond' and 'lightning'. The symbol of unbreakable consciousness. Fundamental tool of tantric rituals.
symbolism: Both ends symbolic — one destruction (burns ignorance), one creation (opens wisdom). Pairs with the bell (chana = emptiness, vajra = form).
dreamMeaning: A diamond in a dream, an unbreakable object, lightning from the sky is the Vajra image. There is an unchanging consciousness inside.
wakingMeaning: A moment when you are shaken? Vajra says: inside is an unbreakable essence. Remember it.
advice: Today find an 'inner truth' that doesn't change even in shaking. Write a sentence: 'Whatever happens I...'

name: Ankh — Key of Life
essence: The Ankh is Egypt's 'life' key. The gods hold it in their hands. The body of the buried-dead pharaoh too — life beyond death.
symbolism: Upper loop masculine-feminine union, lower bar descent to the world. The passage of immortal life through two layers.
dreamMeaning: An Ankh in a dream, attaining life, the symbolic power of a key is the Ankh image. The life force inside you is awakening.
wakingMeaning: Is your life energy low? Ankh says: there is an immortal source inside you — call it.
advice: Today take a small step to say 'I am alive' — barefoot on the ground, deep breath, smile.

name: Caduceus — Hermes's Staff
essence: The Caduceus is a staff with two snakes wrapped around, with wings on top. The symbol of Hermes (messenger god). The ancestor of the modern medical logo.
symbolism: Two snakes = balancing of opposites (masculine-feminine, protection-danger). The wings the speed of communication, the message's carrying.
dreamMeaning: Two snakes wrapped around a staff in a dream, a winged object is the Caduceus image. There is a balance of opposing energies inside.
wakingMeaning: Do you carry the balance between two opposite powers? Caduceus says: you can unite both — the symbol is in your hand.
advice: Today balance in a 'communication' process — both speak and listen, both give and take.

name: Endless Knot
essence: The endless knot is one of the 8 Buddhist auspicious symbols. A knot with no beginning or end — endless bond, cycle of cause and effect.
symbolism: Everything is connected to everything. A single move causes thousands of consequences. The visual form of karma.
dreamMeaning: Connected knots in a dream, the inseparable togetherness of two things is the Endless Knot image. You recognise a bond.
wakingMeaning: Are you trying to break a bond? Endless Knot says: bonds continue in the unconscious. Understanding from the root is essential.
advice: Today think of a bond you have long 'severed'. Are there still effects? Recognise consciously.

name: Cornucopia — Horn of Abundance
essence: The Cornucopia — a horn full of fruit, vegetables, flowers. In Greek, the horn of the goat Amalthea that nursed Zeus. Symbol of unlimited abundance.
symbolism: A source the giving side never depletes. The generosity of nature. Bonded with gratitude.
dreamMeaning: A bowl full of fruit in a dream, a harvest scene, an abundance scene is the Cornucopia image. Abundance flows in your life.
wakingMeaning: Are you in abundance right now? Cornucopia says: carry with gratitude. Otherwise you won't notice.
advice: Today think of 5 'abundances' — small or large, doesn't matter. Gratitude is the beginning of abundance.

name: Olive Branch
essence: The olive branch is Athena's gift to Athens, the figure on Noah's dove's beak, the figure on the UN flag. Universal peace symbol.
symbolism: The olive tree lives hundreds of years — peace too is long-lived. Extending the branch says both 'not weapon' and 'I want continuity'.
dreamMeaning: An olive branch in a dream, a dove, a peace office is the Olive Branch image. A reconciliation is near.
wakingMeaning: Do you want to reconcile in a bond? Olive Branch says: you make the first move. The symbol is the realiser.
advice: Today send a small message to a 'estranged' person — even just 'good morning' suffices.

name: Triple Moon
essence: Triple Moon symbol — opening half-moon (Maiden), full moon (Mother), closing half-moon (Crone). The moon-cycle state of the triple goddess.
symbolism: Three phases of women's life. The moon as a nature cycle. The central symbol of Wicca and modern feminine spirituality.
dreamMeaning: Three different moon phases in a dream, three women in three ages is the Triple Moon image. You question which phase of life cycle you are in.
wakingMeaning: Which phase are you in life — Maiden (potential), Mother (production), Crone (wisdom)? Triple Moon says: all three are valuable, all three are your parts.
advice: Today ask which moon phase your body feels in. Young, producing, wise? Don't look at age — look at feeling.

name: Aleph — First Letter
essence: Aleph is the first letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Numerical value 1 — unity. In the Kabbalist tradition the breath of God, the first sound of creation.
symbolism: Makes no sound — a 'silent' letter. The beginning of everything that exists comes from silence. In Borges's story 'Aleph' the entire universe at one point.
dreamMeaning: A letter in a dream, a silent beginning, the moment of the first movement is the Aleph image. Everything is here, not yet opened.
wakingMeaning: Are you at the threshold of a new beginning? Aleph says: the first sound comes from silence. First pause, then begin.
advice: Today before a beginning stand 1 minute in silence. Silence is pre-word.

name: Sri Yantra — Hindu Sacred Geometry
essence: Sri Yantra is 9 interlocking triangles — 4 upward (masculine / Shiva), 5 downward (feminine / Shakti). The most complex sacred geometric form. The centre is a single point.
symbolism: The tantric union of masculine-feminine. From outer rings to centre (bindu) — the whole universe unfolds from this point and folds into it.
dreamMeaning: A complex geometric pattern in a dream, a point at the centre, triangles is the Sri Yantra image. You are on a deep integration journey.
wakingMeaning: Is there a topic that seems complex? Sri Yantra says: in every complexity is a centre — meditate toward it.
advice: Today break a subject into '9 layers'. Then see how each layer connects to a single point in the centre.

name: Chakra — Consciousness Wheels
essence: Chakra (Sanskrit 'wheel') refers to 7 main energy centres along the body. From root to crown — Muladhara, Svadhisthana, Manipura, Anahata, Vishuddha, Ajna, Sahasrara.
symbolism: Each chakra is bound to a colour, an element, a life theme. If blocked it gives both physical and psychological symptoms. The basis of Kundalini yoga.
dreamMeaning: Coloured lights in your body in a dream, motion along the spine, lotus flowers — Chakra image. The energy body is awakening.
wakingMeaning: Is there tightness in some area of your body? Chakra says: an 'energy centre' is working there — emotional as well as physical.
advice: Today do a 5-minute meditation scanning your body. Start from root, go to crown. Which chakra wants to speak?

name: Pentagram — Five-Pointed Star
essence: The pentagram is a five-pointed star — long history from Pythagoras to Wicca. Forms the human figure of Vitruvian Man.
symbolism: Five corners — earth, water, air, fire, spirit (5th element). Point up = spirit ruling matter; inverted = matter covering spirit.
dreamMeaning: A five-pointed star in a dream, the union of 5 points is the Pentagram image. A call to unite five elements.
wakingMeaning: Is an element missing in your life? Pentagram says: scan all 5. Earth (body), water (feeling), air (mind), fire (will), spirit (meaning).
advice: Today spend 5 minutes with each element: step on earth, drink water, breathe, set an intention, a moment of meditation.

name: Single Eye — All-Seeing Eye
essence: A single eye inside a triangle — the symbol of divine sight. Based on Egypt's Horus eye, in Christian iconography 'the eye of God', common in Masonry.
symbolism: Triangle — Father/Son/Holy Spirit or Body/Mind/Spirit. Eye — the consciousness of being 'seen'. The hidden becomes visible.
dreamMeaning: An eye in the middle of a triangle in a dream, feeling watched is the All-Seeing Eye image. You are under divine gaze.
wakingMeaning: Are you in an area where you thought you are unseen? All-Seeing Eye says: real sight is internal — and you are seen.
advice: Today think of something you did privately. You see it — this is enough awareness.
